aliases: Din Tai Fung Cocoa Wafer Bun

english: Din Tai Fung Cocoa Wafer Bun

food_subgroup: Filled steamed buns (baozi) — sweet cocoa dough with wafer-cream filling and hazelnut topping

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general steamed bun category (FOODON_03302050). No specific code exists for cocoa-flavoured filled steamed bun with wafer filling and hazelnut topping; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers steamed bread and buns as a category. No specific code for cocoa wafer-filled bao exists; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific filled steamed bun product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 cocoa wafer buns, each approximately 40 g, totalling approximately 120 g as served at Din Tai Fung Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Steamed Bun, Sweet Filled (Baozi, sweet) — adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and NCC Food and Nutrient Database category anchor for cocoa-enriched sweet steamed bun with fat-based filling
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy; NCC Food and Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for cocoa wafer bun exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are estimated by compositing: (1) USDA SR Legacy 'Bread, steamed, Chinese style' as the dough base, (2) USDA SR Legacy chocolate wafer cookie data for the wafer-cream filling component, and (3) USDA SR Legacy hazelnut data for the topping. Proportions estimated as approximately 60% dough, 30% wafer-cream filling, 10% hazelnut topping by weight. Energy and macros cross-checked against NCC category anchor for sweet filled steamed buns. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Steamed bread / bao — analogous GI estimate based on 'Steamed bread, white' (GI 68, glucose reference) and 'Chocolate cake with chocolate frosting' component adjustment
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for cocoa wafer bun. GI estimated at 65 based on: (1) University of Sydney GI Database entry for steamed white bread/bao (GI ~68, glucose scale); (2) downward adjustment to approximately 65 reflecting fat and sugar content from wafer-cream filling, which moderates glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 57.0) / 100 = 37.1, where carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 47.5 × 1.2 = 57.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg/100 g; ~252 mg per 3-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit this item to occasional consumption, as a 3-piece serving contributes approximately 252 mg sodium (~11% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), and portion control is advised when combined with other sodium-containing dishes.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (GL ~37) driven by refined wheat flour dough and sugar-rich wafer-cream filling
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this item as an occasional indulgence only; the estimated glycemic load of approximately 37 per 3-piece serving is high and will produce a significant postprandial blood glucose rise, requiring careful carbohydrate budgeting within the meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.2 g/100 g) and added sugar (~16 g/100 g) from wafer-cream filling
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, this bun should be consumed sparingly; the saturated fat from the chocolate wafer filling and the high added sugar content both contribute to atherogenic risk, and a single 3-piece serving provides approximately 5 g saturated fat, approaching 25% of the recommended daily limit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Cocoa Wafer Bun (3 pieces), known in Chinese as 巧克力威化包 (Qiǎokèlì Wēihuà Bāo), is a signature sweet steamed bun served at Din Tai Fung restaurants in Singapore and internationally. The bun is made from a soft, fluffy cocoa-enriched wheat dough that is leavened with yeast and steamed to a pillowy texture. Inside, it is filled with a rich chocolate wafer cream spread — a smooth, sweet filling reminiscent of hazelnut-chocolate wafer biscuits — and the exterior is finished with a scattering of crushed hazelnuts that add a gentle crunch and subtle nuttiness. Each order consists of three individual buns, each weighing approximately 40 g, for a total serving weight of around 120 g. Nutritionally, the bun is energy-dense at approximately 318 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content (~6.8 g/100 g), significant carbohydrate (~47.5 g/100 g) from refined wheat flour and added sugars, and meaningful fat (~11.5 g/100 g) from the wafer-cream filling and hazelnut topping. The estimated glycemic index is 65 (medium) and the glycemic load per serving is high (~37), making it a food that requires careful portion management for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The saturated fat content (~4.2 g/100 g) and added sugar (~16 g/100 g) are relevant considerations for cardiovascular health. This item is vegetarian but contains gluten, dairy, and tree nuts (hazelnuts), making allergen awareness important. It is best understood as a dessert-style dim sum item rather than a staple grain food, despite its classification in the grains and staples group.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for cocoa wafer bun exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods, or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated by compositional modelling from USDA SR Legacy components (steamed Chinese bread, chocolate wafer cookie, hazelnut). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database steamed bread entry. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-characterised in approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data (steamed Chinese-style bread, chocolate wafer cookie, hazelnut) in approximate proportions of 60:30:10 by weight. No exact branded entry was available in approved sources as of the access date. 2. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database steamed white bread entry (GI ~68) with a modest downward adjustment for fat and sugar content of the filling. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data used for estimation. 4. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data used for estimation. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data used for estimation. 6. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data used for estimation. 7. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data used for estimation. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source data used for estimation. 9. vitamin_c_mg is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero as cocoa wafer bun contains no vitamin C-contributing ingredients. 10. Halal status is listed as false because Din Tai Fung Singapore is not halal-certified as of the access date; consumers should verify current certification status. 11. The food_group 'Grains and staples' is preserved from the input record as the primary dough base is wheat flour, though this item functions nutritionally as a dessert. 12. trans_fat_g of 0.1 g/100 g reflects possible trace trans fats from partially hydrogenated oils in commercial wafer cream; this is an estimate and may be 0 in reformulated products.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full record from scratch. Estimated nutrients from USDA SR Legacy component data. Estimated GI from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry. Added all mandatory Singaporean-cuisine multilingual names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Confirmed vitamin_c_mg = 0.0 is justified (no vitamin C source ingredients). Verified all ontology match_type values use only allowed strings. Verified GI > 0 (65). All hard gates and Q1-Q8 criteria satisfied on first iteration.
